commands.title: /execute
commands.description: The Swiss Army knife of commands. Changes execution context.
commands.breakdown: Modern Bedrock syntax uses: as, at, positioned, rotated, if block/entity, unless block/entity, and run.

examples.label: Check Block
examples.cmd: /execute if block ~ ~-1 ~ air run fill ~ ~-1 ~ ~ ~-1 ~ stone

commands.title: /execute as
commands.description: Changes the 'who' is running the command.
commands.breakdown: The command runs with the permissions and identity of the target.

commands.title: /execute at
commands.description: Changes the 'where' the command is running.
commands.breakdown: Sets the execution coordinates to the current position of the target.

commands.title: execute if
commands.description: Conditional execution if a specific condition is met.

commands.title: execute unless
commands.description: Conditional execution if a specific condition is NOT met.
